# When to Use A/B Testing

Use A/B testing when:

- you can randomize traffic,
- variants are few,
- the metric is defined,
- enough sample exists,
- you need a clean comparison.

Do not use when:

- traffic is tiny,
- many variants need exploration,
- conversion delay is too long,
- platform delivery will not split evenly.

Use bandits when learning-while-earning matters more than a clean fixed-horizon comparison.
